Pathological analysis revealed that the pathological types included pleural mesothelioma and lymphoepithelioma-like carcinoma, further demonstrating the complexity of the patient's disease. At the time of discharge, the patient's condition was stable and continued outpatient treatment and follow-up were required. instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format. Input report:
input: The patient, a 46-year-old female, was admitted to the hospital on January 19, 2019 due to brain metastasis. After admission, a complete evaluation was performed, and the medical history was collected on April 18, 2018. The pathological confirmation date was November 4, 2018, and the first diagnosis of lung cancer was May 19, 2024. This hospitalization mainly underwent thoracic perfusion treatment, which started on August 17, 2023 and ended on September 15, 2023. The patient's pathological types were solid adenocarcinoma and colloid adenocarcinoma, and there were malignant tumors with tumor thrombi. In terms of hereditary tumor markers, BRCA1, BRCA2, STK11 and RB1 were all positive. The patient also had genetic changes of MET amplification, HER3 (ERBB3), HER2 (ERBB2) 20 insertion positivity and ALK amplification. KRAS detection showed G12 and G12C, and BRAF showed V600E mutation. The FGFR test results showed rearrangement and point mutation, and the RET test results showed point mutation and deletion. The tumor immune environment assessment showed that the PDL1 expression value was 68%, the Tumor Proportion Score (TPS) was 10%, and the Immunocyte Score (IC) was 35, indicating the presence of immune activity. In addition, the patient's ECOG score was 4, indicating that the patient's condition was serious and his daily life was severely restricted. The patient's complications included digestive system obstruction, circulatory system obstruction, Guillain-Barré syndrome of the endocrine and immune systems, infectious disease hepatitis A, and genitourinary system bleeding. The patient's brain metastasis site was a secondary malignant tumor in the brain, and the date of brain metastasis was April 2, 2018. Eye, ear, nose and throat related diseases were bleeding. Drugs for lung cancer were used during the treatment, and the first treatment time was June 24, 2024. After a series of treatments, the patient is currently in a stable condition and is ready for discharge. Discharge instructions include regular follow-up, drug treatment, rest, a balanced diet, and necessary rehabilitation training. Subsequent treatment recommendations are adjusted based on the review results. json results:

input: The patient, male, was born on September 28, 1976, and was admitted to the hospital on May 1, 2019 due to lung discomfort. After detailed examination, it was confirmed to be adenocarcinoma in situ and squamous cell carcinoma in situ. The patient's medical history was complicated, including respiratory diseases such as emphysema, digestive system diseases such as obstruction, genitourinary system diseases such as hypertension and nephropathy, eye, ear, nose and throat related diseases such as iritis, endocrine and immune system diseases such as myasthenia gravis, neurological diseases such as bipolar disorder, and a series of other malignant tumors, such as liver cancer. The patient underwent the first lung resection on November 8, 2018, and then the first imaging diagnosis was made on November 1, 2020, and the pathological diagnosis was made on June 9, 2019. On November 28, 2023, a pathological examination was performed again, and it was confirmed to be spindle cell carcinoma. Immunoassays showed that TP53, KRAS, MET, RB1, BRAF V600E, and HER4 (ERBB4) were all positive. EGFR mutations include L861Q and C797S. RET gene testing showed positive fusion. NTRK test results showed that NTRK1, NTRK2, and NTRK3 were positive, and fusion and point mutations were found. Tumor PD-L1 expression detection showed TPS of 10% and CPS of 15%. The immune cell (IC) score was 20%. FGFR4 gene rearrangement occurred. Based on these molecular characteristics, the patient received targeted drug therapy. The treatment involves drugs including targeted drugs for gene mutations such as EGFR, BRAF, MET, NTRK, and cell therapy. The treatment started on June 18, 2021 and ended on May 18, 2020. During this hospitalization, the patient received careful treatment and care from the medical team. His condition is currently stable, his ECOG score is good, and he meets the conditions for discharge. According to the patient's condition changes and treatment response, a detailed post-discharge follow-up plan and home care instructions have been formulated. instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format. Input report:
input: The patient, a female, was born on July 9, 1981. She was admitted to the hospital on November 29, 2018 because she was diagnosed with sarcomatoid carcinoma with liver and cerebral hemisphere metastasis. Genetic testing showed KRAS mutation (G213, Q61L), FGFR1 positive, EGFR exon 19 positive, MET exon 14 skipping mutation, TP53 positive, HER4 (ERBB4) positive, and HER2 (ERBB2) without exon 20 insertion. The patient's ECOG performance status was 2 and was evaluated on May 21, 2021. The patient also carried NTRK-fusion. In addition, the patient suffered from multiple underlying diseases, including stroke, hypertensive nephropathy, diabetes, human papillomavirus infection, empyema, and digestive system obstruction. The patient's first symptoms appeared on June 15, 2022, the first imaging diagnosis was confirmed on August 17, 2019, and the first treatment began on June 29, 2019, including surgical treatment. During hospitalization, the patient received a personalized treatment plan, including targeted drug therapy and surgical treatment tailored to the characteristics of his disease. Immunoassays found that the PDL1 expression level was at a low level, the IC score was also low, and the CPS score did not exceed the medium level, which may have affected the patient's response to immunotherapy. Based on the patient's clinical manifestations, imaging examinations, genetic testing, and immune test results, we adopted a plan of surgery as the main treatment supplemented by targeted drug therapy. Based on the patient's clinical manifestations, genetic test results and treatment response, the patient was given a combination treatment of pazopanib and the immune checkpoint inhibitor pembrolizumab. The treatment ended on June 10, 2024, and the patient's condition was stable with no obvious adverse reactions. The patient first experienced lung cancer-related symptoms on June 5, 2024, and has now met the conditions for discharge. It is recommended that patients follow up regularly and pay attention to tumor marker levels and imaging test results to assess disease progression and treatment efficacy. After discharge, continue to take pazopanib orally and receive pembrolizumab treatment regularly, while paying attention to observing and managing potential side effects. instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format. Input report:
input: The patient, a 57-year-old male, was admitted to the hospital for chest pain and dyspnea. The patient was diagnosed with lung cancer for the first time after imaging examination in our hospital 3 years ago (March 5, 2020) due to cough and blood in sputum. The pathological diagnosis was obtained by puncture surgery on April 20, 2022, and the results showed colloid adenocarcinoma with solid adenocarcinoma features. Since then, the patient has received a series of treatments, including the first lung resection on March 5, 2023, and an imaging review on September 13, 2022, which confirmed the presence of pericardial perfusion in the tumor and believed that the disease had progressed to the late stage. The patient has multiple gene mutations, including ALK point mutations and fusions, BRCA1 positivity, BRAF V600E, and other rare mutations in genes such as EGFR, KRAS, KEAP1, RB1, STK11, and MET. Genetic testing showed that NTRK1, NTRK2, and NTRK3 were positive, with fusion and rearrangement characteristics. The PDL1 expression value was 57%, suggesting the possible effect of immunotherapy. The patient also has multiple comorbidities such as glaucoma, cirrhosis, severe renal insufficiency, hepatitis A, and superior vena cava tamponade syndrome, which require special attention during treatment. Considering the patient's ECOG score of 1, urogenital dysfunction, and high expression of PDL1 levels, it was decided to use a combination of targeted therapy and immunotherapy. The treatment drugs will be based on standard lung cancer drugs, combined with individualized medications based on the patient's genetic test results and other health conditions. instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format. Input report:
input: The patient, a 65-year-old male, was admitted to the hospital for "repeated coughing and expectoration for 1 year, which worsened with chest pain for 2 weeks". The patient had coughing and white foamy sputum for 1 year ago, without obvious inducement, accompanied by intermittent chest pain, which was dull in nature and was not taken seriously. Two weeks ago, the cough and chest pain symptoms worsened, the amount of sputum increased, and the sputum was bloody, so he came to our hospital for treatment. He had a history of lung cancer (non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma combined with solid adenocarcinoma), which was pathologically diagnosed on October 19, 2019. He underwent left upper lobectomy on December 20, 2019, and received chemotherapy after surgery. There was a case of renal cancer in the family history. The patient's genetic test showed that the EGFR 19 mutation was positive, ALK and ROS1 rearrangement were positive, and KRAS, BRAF (V600E mutation), TP53, BRCA, KEAP1, STK11, RB1, and HER3 were positive. NTRK gene test showed that NTRK1 was positive. FGFR gene was positive. The PD-L1 expression level was 65%, the CPS score was 45, and the IC score was 50. Physical examination upon admission: body temperature 36.5℃, pulse 80 beats/min, respiration 20 times/min, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg; dry and wet rales were audible on lung auscultation. The patient adapted well to nasogastric feeding, without nausea and vomiting. The inflammation of the genitourinary system was relieved, and there were no symptoms of urgency or frequency of urination. The symptoms of peripheral neuritis improved, and there was no obvious numbness of the limbs. The symptoms of hyperparathyroidism were under stable control. The patient continued to receive treatment for lung cancer, and the current maintenance medication is gefitinib, which is aimed at EGFR mutations. input: The patient, a 78-year-old male, was admitted to the hospital on 2023-04-12 due to "repeated coughing, dyspnea, and discomfort in the brain." NTRK test was positive, and its fusion, point mutation, and rearrangement characteristics need to be further clarified. Lung CT scan revealed that the tumor ruptured and involved blood vessels. In addition, the patient also had brain metastasis, which was confirmed on 2022-02-26, and the head discomfort was relieved after treatment. Based on the above medical data and clinical manifestations, an individualized treatment plan was developed for the patient, including targeted therapy for EGFR mutations (such as gefitinib) and immunotherapy (such as pembrolizumab), and treatment began on 2021-01-11. input: Admission record: Patient Li, male, 60 years old, was admitted to the hospital due to cough and chest pain, and the symptoms lasted for 3 months. He was previously healthy, but had a clear history of hypertension and nephropathy. The father in the family was diagnosed with lung cancer at the age of 68 and the mother at the age of 63. The patient has a long history of smoking. Physical examination at admission: body temperature 36.8℃, heart rate 80 beats/min, respiratory rate 20 times/min, blood pressure 150/90mmHg. He was conscious, dry and wet rales were audible in the right lower lobe of the lung, the heart rhythm was regular, and no murmurs were heard. Blood routine: white blood cell, red blood cell and platelet counts were all within the normal range. A lung CT scan was performed on July 3, 2020, and the results showed that abnormal density shadows were visible in the right lower lobe of the lung. The patient also had respiratory diseases with hemothorax. The date of medical history collection was September 29, 2021, and the first imaging diagnosis was July 3, 2020. Genetic testing was performed on July 22, 2021, and the results showed KRAS Q61 mutation, BRAF V600E mutation, EGFR exon 20 insertion, MET exon 14 insertion and MET gene fusion, TP53 positive, RB1 positive and HER3 (ERBB3) positive. Immunoassay was completed on May 17, 2021, with PD-L1 expression of 14%, TPS of 8%, and CPS of 66. The first treatment started on January 29, 2021, and the treatment included drugs Aminrun, cyclophosphamide and docetaxel. The end date of treatment was June 6, 2021. The patient had undergone surgical intervention at the site of brain metastasis, which occurred on April 13, 2021. The brain metastasis included meningeal metastasis but no other brain metastasis. The patient's pathological type was thymic carcinoma, which was confirmed by the diagnosing physician Li Liu. In addition, the patient has endocrine and immune system diseases such as gout, eye, ear, nose and throat related diseases recorded as "perforation", genitourinary system disease history including hypertensive nephropathy, circulatory system diseases including superior vena cava tamponade syndrome, and infectious diseases such as syphilis spirochetes infection. Malignant tumors show that the patient has leukemia. Based on the above rich medical data and detailed clinical records, we will comprehensively consider the patient's overall situation and carefully formulate a targeted treatment plan. json results:

input: Discharge records: The patient was admitted on March 14, 2020, and was subsequently discharged on June 6, 2021. The past medical history was significant, including rheumatoid arthritis, luminal stenosis, AIDS, hepatitis A, and renal anemia. The patient's ECOG score was 2 during physical examination, indicating that the patient's self-care ability was limited. Imaging examinations revealed brain and liver metastases. Pathological examination confirmed the name of the disease as adenoid cystic carcinoma, and the pathological type was giant cell carcinoma. The patient received radiotherapy and underwent the first lung resection on January 2, 2021. The chemotherapy regimen included paclitaxel (Taol) combined with carboplatin, and the specific dose and frequency were adjusted according to the patient's condition, but were not mentioned in the record. The patient responded well to chemotherapy, and the efficacy evaluation showed that the lung tumor was relieved to a certain extent. At the time of discharge, the patient had good self-care ability. The follow-up plan includes monthly review of tumor markers and imaging examinations every three months to monitor disease progression and evaluate treatment efficacy. The diagnostic physician is Li Liu. The medical history information was collected on September 29, 2021, the first confirmed disease date was March 14, 2020, the pathology date was recorded on August 19, 2021, and the ECOG score was recorded on March 24, 2021. Treatment started on May 1, 2020 and ended on June 6, 2021. The malignant tumor was diagnosed as non-Hodgkin's lymphoma. The record date is February 9, 2021. input: Patient basic information: Name: Li Liu, age: 40 years old, admission date: 2020-02-06. It was diagnosed as papillary adenocarcinoma, the pathological type was adenocarcinoma, and the pathological diagnosis time was 2020-11-13. After immunoassay, the test date was 2021-05-17, and the results included MET fusion, ROS1 fusion, CPS value of 66, TPS of 8%, EGFR L861Q mutation, RET deletion, NTRK point mutation, NTRK3 positive, HER4 (ERBB4) positive, STK11 positive, KRAS positive. The patient also had other concomitant diseases, including renal failure in genitourinary system diseases, psoriasis in endocrine and immune system diseases, obstructive pulmonary emphysema in respiratory system diseases, human papillomavirus infection in infectious diseases, and Parkinson's disease in neurological diseases. The patient underwent the first tumor resection surgery on 2021-01-02, and the surgical site was the lung. During the treatment, the patient received endocrine therapy and used drugs including Aminrun, cyclophosphamide, and docetaxel. Discharge: The patient's condition has stabilized after treatment, his vital signs are normal, and his symptoms have been relieved. He was discharged on 2021-03-04. The ECOG score is 1, and the score date is 2021-03-24. Discharge instructions: The patient should continue to regularly review the lung lesions while at home, and follow the doctor's instructions to standardize medication. The dosage and frequency of medication include Aminrun 100mg, twice a day; cyclophosphamide 50mg, once a day; docetaxel 150mg, once a week, on Thursday or Friday. On 2020-11-13, it was diagnosed as mixed invasive mucinous and non-mucinous adenocarcinoma by puncture examination. During the treatment, the patient underwent lung resection surgery, and specifically performed right upper lobectomy on 2021-01-02, the first lung resection time of the disease. After the operation, the chemotherapy regimen of Aminrun, cyclophosphamide and docetaxel was used, with a specific dose of once every 21 days, a total of 6 times, and the end date of treatment was 2021-06-06. During this period, the patient experienced severe arrhythmias and pulmonary embolism, which were confirmed and properly treated in electrophysiological examinations and pulmonary angiography, respectively. Brain metastasis occurred on 2021-04-13, and the patient received corresponding neurological treatment. Immunoassays on 2021-05-17 showed a PDL1 level of 14% and a TPS of 8%, which indicate that immunotherapy may have some efficacy. Genetic testing revealed that STK11, HER2 (ERBB2), TP53, BRCA, HER4 (ERBB4), KRAS were positive or amplified, EGFR had a 19del/L858R mutation, ROS1 had an S1986F mutation, MET showed MET14 deletion, and NTRK3 and RET gene insertions. These genetic characteristics help guide individualized medical decisions. The patient was also diagnosed with systemic diseases such as severe arrhythmias, pulmonary embolism, gout, and dementia, which require continued attention and management. According to the pathology record dated 2021-08-19, the patient's condition is stable. At the time of discharge, patients are advised to have regular reviews and adjust their treatment plans according to clinical conditions, including but not limited to adjusting medications based on genetic test results and immune status, monitoring heart rhythm and embolic conditions, and appropriate management of neurological diseases. json results:

input: The patient, a 40-year-old woman, had mixed non-small cell lung cancer and was first diagnosed on 2020-03-14. Pathological examination showed that the tumor was adenoid cystic carcinoma, and the histological type was moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. The medical history included hypertensive nephropathy and heart failure, and a history of digestive system diseases with gastrointestinal fistulas and respiratory diseases with atelectasis. In addition, the patient had a history of bleeding eye, ear, nose and throat related diseases and infectious diseases AIDS. The main complaint of this admission was dyspnea, and the physical examination revealed atelectasis. Detailed auxiliary examination results showed that the tumor had metastasized to the liver, and brain metastases were found on 2021-04-13. During the subsequent treatment, the patient received liver interventional treatment and was first pathologically confirmed on 2020-11-13. The treatment drugs included Aminrun, cyclophosphamide and docetaxel, and the treatment started on 2020-05-01 and ended on 2021-06-06. The results of immunoassays showed that the patient's HER3 (ERBB3), ALK, HER4 (ERBB4), KEAP1, EGFR mutations G719 and 18del, NTRK1, MET amplification, ROS1 mutations G3032R and S1986Y, STK11, and TP53 were all positive, indicating that the tumor is at risk of recurrence and metastasis. HER2 (ERBB2) 20 insertion mutation was also positive in pathological examination. The ECOG score was 1, indicating that the patient still maintained good living ability. The same positive result was observed in the immunoassay on 2021-05-17. In view of the patient's RET rearrangement and deletion, his discharge diagnosis was mixed non-small cell lung cancer, liver and brain metastases, and severe heart failure. The discharge doctor recommended that the patient continue chemotherapy and undergo regular review and follow-up to monitor the progression of the disease and the effectiveness of treatment. json results:

input: Patient name: Zhang San, gender: female, age: 40 years old, hospital number: 12345. The patient was admitted to the hospital on February 6, 2020 due to persistent cough and dyspnea. Physical examination revealed weakened breath sounds and was initially diagnosed with lung cancer. After admission, the disease was confirmed to be colloid adenocarcinoma based on the results of pathological examination, and the first pathological diagnosis (puncture, postoperative pathology, etc.) was obtained on November 13, 2020. The patient began chemotherapy on January 29, 2021, using paclitaxel, with a dose adjusted according to body weight and tumor burden, once every three weeks. During chemotherapy, the patient's ECOG score was 1, indicating that the patient was able to perform daily activities but not heavy physical labor. In the ECOG evaluation on March 24, 2021, lung cancer metastasized to the brain, including meningeal metastases and brainstem metastases. MRI head scans revealed these metastatic areas. Genetic testing showed that PDL1 expression was 14%, TPS was 8%, and the patient also had positive results for STK11, RB1, TP53, BRCA, as well as FGFR fusion, translocation, NTRK point mutation, and NTRK2 gene abnormalities. In addition, the patient also suffered from other diseases, such as ulcers in eye, ear, nose and throat related diseases, circulatory system diseases including occlusion and atherosclerosis, infectious diseases such as AIDS, nervous system diseases such as bipolar disorder, genitourinary system diseases such as renal anemia and renal cysts, endocrine and immune system diseases such as psoriasis, and malignant tumors such as leukemia. After comprehensive treatment, the CP value (i.e., comprehensive positive score) was 66. The disease progressed rapidly and was unstable. Finally, the patient was discharged on August 19, 2021 with a diagnosis of advanced colloid adenocarcinoma. The surgical record showed that the patient had previously undergone surgery involving liver metastasis. The specific method of tumor treatment included stem cell transplantation, and the treatment started on May 1, 2020. The patient tolerated chemotherapy well, but mild to moderate adverse reactions occurred, including nausea and hair loss. When the patient was discharged from the hospital, the diagnosing doctor, Li Liu, suggested that the patient continue chemotherapy and have regular follow-up visits to monitor the progress of the disease. The patient also had concomitant diseases such as peripheral neuritis and softening. According to the above comprehensive situation, the patient has been arranged to undergo surgery to remove the tumor and undergo radiotherapy and chemotherapy. The drug treatment plan is: Aminrun 20 mg orally daily, cyclophosphamide 200 mg/m2 body surface area intravenous injection, docetaxel 75 mg/m2 body surface area intravenous injection, repeated every 3 weeks, a total of 6 courses. The hospitalized physician is Dr. Li Liu, and the medical history was collected on September 29, 2021. The discharge plan date is September 15, 2022. json results:

Admission diagnosis: The patient was first diagnosed with mucoepidermoid carcinoma on 2020-03-14, accompanied by malignant tumors of the liver and kidneys. Liver cancer is located in the right lobe of the lung, the tumor size is 3cm×2cm, and the pathological type is pleomorphic carcinoma. Kidney cancer is located in the left kidney, the tumor size is 4cm×3cm, and it is also pleomorphic carcinoma. The results of molecular marker detection showed: HER2 (ERBB2) non-20 insertion type, CPS score of 66, KEAP1 positive, FGFR detected FGFR1 expression, NTRK showed NTRK2 positive, STK11 positive, ALK gene fusion, EGFR showed T790M mutation, HER4 (ERBB4) positive, and tumor programmed cell death protein (TPS) was 8%.

The treatment drugs included Aminrun, cyclophosphamide and docetaxel. The tumor treatment used particle implantation. The treatment ended on 2021-06-06. After treatment, the tumor volume of liver cancer and kidney cancer was reduced, and no new metastases were found.

Surgery and complications: The surgical site was recorded as other. Complications include lung infection (2021-02-15), cancer thrombosis (2021-03-01), nervous system infection (2021-04-10) and mental illness (2021-05-20), as well as endocrine and immune system diseases, such as type 1 diabetes (2021-05-25) and prurigo (2021-06-02). Brain metastasis is unclear.

Condition changes and evaluation: The ECOG score on 2021-03-24 was 1, indicating that the patient has good self-care ability. The discharge diagnosis was mucoepidermoid carcinoma, accompanied by liver cancer and kidney cancer.

Discharge instructions: It is recommended that the patient undergo a quarterly review and continue to take treatment drugs regularly, while paying attention to rest and diet conditioning. Other discharge instructions are not specifically recorded.

Other disease conditions: The patient also has a diagnosis of infectious diseases, specifically cancer thrombosis and AIDS, as well as iritis among eye, ear, nose and throat related diseases. json results:

In addition, the patient had brain metastases, the metastatic site was the brain stem, and the diagnosis date of brain metastasis was 2021-04-13. **Chief complaint and current medical history:**

The patient was admitted to the hospital due to chest pain and dyspnea lasting for about 3 months, and the symptoms worsened in the past month. Since the first diagnosis of lung cancer on February 16, 2021, the patient has experienced a series of changes in his condition. The first symptoms appeared on October 6, 2021. Subsequent examinations found that lung cancer had metastasized to the liver, and brain metastases to the cerebral hemispheres were found on December 18, 2021. The pathological type is clearly thymic carcinoma. Molecular test results showed that the EGFR gene had E709V mutation, the ROS1 gene had G3032R mutation, the RB1 and HER4 (ERBB4) genes were positive, and there was ALK gene fusion and MET gene amplification. Main diagnosis:

- Disease code: ICD-10 C34.9

Admission:
The patient was admitted to the hospital on January 5, 2022 due to chest tightness, cough, and sputum, without hemoptysis or obvious dyspnea. Past medical history includes breast cancer and emphysema, and has undergone breast cancer surgery and chemotherapy. For emphysema, continuous oxygen therapy is received. Physical examination revealed that dry and wet rales were audible in the lungs, the heart rate was normal, and the blood pressure was stable. Auxiliary examinations showed that the lung CT scan showed a lesion with a diameter of about cm, located in the upper lobe of the left lung, with unclear boundaries, and suspected bronchial invasion.

Treatment process:
After consultation with Dr. Geng Qian, it was confirmed that the patient was adenocarcinoma spindle cell type with lung metastasis. A thoracic perfusion treatment plan was formulated, combined with chemotherapy, and the chemotherapy plan was 1 cycle every 21 days, including paclitaxel and cisplatin. During treatment, renal function was closely monitored, and creatinine and urea nitrogen levels were tested once a week. At the same time, the patient received radiotherapy for brain metastasis and underwent surgical treatment for eye stones.

Discharge status:
The discharge diagnosis was confirmed to be adenocarcinoma spindle cell type with lung metastasis. Discharge instructions included continuing treatment according to the existing chemotherapy regimen, one cycle every 21 days, continuing to receive radiotherapy, and regularly monitoring the levels of tumor markers CEA and CA125. After discharge, the patient should continue to receive radiotherapy and monitor the levels of tumor markers.

Summary of diagnosis and treatment:
The effect of this hospitalization treatment was significant, the volume of tumor lesions was reduced, and the patient's condition was effectively controlled. The patient has a positive attitude towards the treatment plan and said that he will continue to follow the doctor's instructions for follow-up treatment.

The staging of the tumor takes into account factors such as tumor size, lymph node metastasis, and the presence of distant metastasis. The patient's ECOG score date is August 23, 2021, with a score of 2, indicating that the patient has a certain degree of mobility but cannot complete all self-care activities.

Based on clinical manifestations, pathological examination and genetic test results, the patient was diagnosed with sarcomatoid carcinoma, which was classified as lung adenocarcinoma in detail.

Admission record: September 20, 2022

1. **Main complaint:**
- The patient was admitted to the hospital for dyspnea, persistent cough and chest pain.

- According to the patient's statement, the symptoms started a few months ago, initially with mild discomfort, but gradually worsened. The patient described that dyspnea worsened significantly after physical activity, chest pain was persistent dull pain, and there was occasional white sticky sputum when coughing.

- Drinking history: Occasionally drinking alcohol.
- Eating habits: Mainly high-fat foods, irregular diet.
- Exercise habits: Lack of regular physical activity.
- Other living habits: Being in a high-pressure working environment for a long time, self-reported poor sleep quality.

- No history of similar diseases in the family, no history of tumors.

- Vital signs: body temperature 36.8°C, heart rate 90 beats/minute, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg.

- Pancreatic cancer, pathological type is adenosquamous carcinoma, with signs of brain metastasis.

- Imaging examination results showed that chest CT revealed multiple lung nodules.
- Pathological examination results support the diagnosis of adenosquamous carcinoma.
- Genetic testing showed ALK positive, HER4 (ERBB4) positive, TP53 positive.

- Taxotere: 150mg, once a day, 30 days of treatment.
- Chenya: 200mg, twice a day, 30 days of treatment.
- Nimustine hydrochloride for injection: 100mg, once every three weeks, for a total of 4 times.
- For immunotherapy: adjusted according to the specific situation of the patient.

10. **Evaluation of treatment effect and patient prognosis:**
- Currently still undergoing treatment, regular review to evaluate the treatment effect.
- Prognosis information will be further provided based on the specific response during treatment.

11. **Diagnostic doctor:**
- Guo Bing, attending physician, oncology department.

Due to the aggressiveness of this pathological type, the patient developed brain metastasis, specifically in the cerebral hemisphere, on November 1, 2021. In addition, the patient also suffered from dry eye, an eye, ear, nose and throat-related disease, Parkinson's disease, a neurological disease, and rheumatoid arthritis, an endocrine and immune system disease. In terms of the cardiovascular system, the patient had a problem with tumor rupture (vascular encapsulation), and digestive system disease obstruction was also a challenge for the patient.

The patient showed a PDL1 of up to 83% in immune testing, a CPS score of 92, and an IC score of 2, showing potential response to immunotherapy. The results of tumor gene testing showed ROS1 mutation S1986Y, TP53 positive, HER3 (ERBB3) positive, ALK positive, KRAS mutation G12V, HER2 (ERBB2) positive, NTRK mutation NTRK2, STK11 positive, and BRAF non-V600 mutation.

Based on the above diagnosis, the treatment plan includes chemotherapy drugs fluorouracil, fluorouracil injection and vindesine. The treatment started on June 7, 2020 and ended on March 17, 2021. The pathological examination date was November 14, 2021, and the ECOG score date was January 27, 2021. The discharge date is scheduled for June 26, 2022.

Because the specific treatment method for the patient's tumor is chemotherapy, the treatment process and drug selection require special attention to ensure the maximization of the treatment effect while controlling side effects. The patient recovered well after the operation without complications.

At the same time, according to the patient's genetic test results, we found that the patient had special conditions such as KRAS (Q61L) gene mutation and HER4 (ERBB4) positivity. The results of the reexamination imaging examination showed that the surgical site recovered well and no obvious abnormalities were found. Combined with the pathological examination (pathological date: 2021-11-04) and other related examination results, the final diagnosis was invasive mucinous adenocarcinoma of the left lower lobe of the lung, and the score is awaiting further evaluation.

Discharge record:

1. **Basic information of the patient:**
- The patient, aged 58, underwent medical history collection for "papillary adenocarcinoma" on December 1, 2021. The confirmed disease name was papillary adenocarcinoma, and the pathological type was colloid adenocarcinoma.

2. **Diagnosis information:**
- During this hospitalization, genetic testing was performed (test date July 13, 2021). The results showed that HER2 (ERBB2), HER3 (ERBB3), HER4 (ERBB4), TP53, RB1 and KEAP1 were all positive, BRAF test results were V600E and non-V600 mutations, EGFR detected G719S mutation, BRCA test was BRCA2 positive, FGFR1 positive. The first pathological diagnosis was on June 15, 2020, and the first imaging diagnosis was on April 3, 2020. In addition, the patient had arterial plaques in the circulatory system, hiatal hernia in the digestive system, inflammation in the urogenital system, and human papillomavirus infection in the infectious disease. The neurological disease was diagnosed as depression, and the brain metastasis was in the brainstem, which occurred on February 18, 2021.

3. **Treatment process:**
- The patient received thoracic perfusion as the specific treatment for the tumor, and the first lung resection was on September 29, 2021. The results of the immunoassay showed that PDL1 was 35%, TPS was 97%, the immunoassay date was June 22, 2021, and IC was 1. During the treatment, drugs such as oxaliplatin, Yimai Lin, and hydroxycamptothecin injection were used, and the treatment ended on April 12, 2022.

4. **Medication record:**
- The patient used oxaliplatin, Yimai Lin, and hydroxycamptothecin injection during the treatment. The drug dosage, frequency, and course of treatment were adjusted according to the patient's specific situation and tolerance. The treatment effect is evaluated by the improvement of clinical symptoms and the relief of imaging examinations, and the drug efficacy is judged to be good.

5. **Discharge status:**
- At discharge, the patient's symptoms and signs are improved compared with those at admission. Specifically, the degree of dyspnea is reduced and brainstem symptoms are controlled. The patient tolerates the current treatment plan well and has no obvious adverse reactions.

6. **Follow-up plan:**
- Patients need to be followed up regularly, including but not limited to tumor marker determination, imaging examination, evaluation of neurological symptoms and psychological status evaluation. The follow-up frequency is recommended to be once every 3 months, pay attention to the recurrence or metastasis of the tumor, and adjust the treatment plan in time.

7. **Diagnostic doctor**:
- This discharge record is filled in and reviewed and confirmed by the attending physician, Dr. Jiang Xu.

The patient mainly showed persistent dyspnea and chest pain at admission, accompanied by cough, sputum and other symptoms.

Past history
The patient had a history of uremia, severe arrhythmia, lung tumors, etc. Uremia was treated with dialysis, arrhythmia was treated with pacemaker implantation, and lung tumors were treated with chemotherapy and surgery. In addition, the patient had a history of hepatitis A and hepatitis B.

Admission diagnosis
The patient had symptoms such as dyspnea and chest pain at admission. Imaging examinations showed multiple masses in the lungs, and the initial diagnosis was diffuse idiopathic pulmonary neuroendocrine cell hyperplasia.

Hospitalization process
The patient underwent surgery for left upper lobe lung cancer on June 12, 2021. The operation was successful and the patient recovered well after the operation. The test results showed that PDL1 was 30%, MET was c-MET amplification, HER2 was non-20 insertion and positive, STK11 was positive, BRCA1 and BRCA2 were both positive, BRAF was non-V600, ROS1 was S1986F, RET was a point mutation, and RB1 was positive. The pathological type was squamous cell carcinoma, CPS was 63, and TPS was 26%. The ECOG score was 1 and IC was 2.

Treatment
During the hospitalization, the patient received surgical treatment, chemotherapy and targeted drug therapy, using drugs such as pingyangmycin and shidasai.

Discharge diagnosis
The patient was diagnosed with diffuse idiopathic pulmonary neuroendocrine cell hyperplasia, the pathological type was squamous cell carcinoma, and the surgical site was brain metastasis (thalamic metastasis).

Discharge instructions
The doctor advised the patient to pay attention to rest, avoid fatigue, continue to take medication on time, and have regular follow-up visits. It is recommended to avoid eating raw, cold and greasy food, keep the indoor air fresh, avoid exposure to pathogenic factors such as smoke and dust, regularly check the lung condition, and take further treatment and rehabilitation measures according to the doctor's advice.
